info.title: ATLAS API
info.description:
Enterprise deployment lifecycle management API with Dynamic Query Builder

## Overview
The ATLAS API provides secure, high-performance endpoints for building and executing dynamic database queries without SQL knowledge.

## Key Features
- **Visual Query Building**: Build complex queries through filter selections
- **SQL Injection Prevention**: All queries use parameterization for security
- **Performance Optimization**: Intelligent caching and query validation
- **Audit Logging**: Complete compliance tracking for all operations
- **Template Support**: Reusable query templates with parameters
- **Export Capabilities**: Export results to CSV, JSON, or Excel

## Authentication
All endpoints require authentication using either:
- **JWT Bearer Token**: For user authentication
- **API Key**: For service-to-service calls (X-API-Key header)

## Rate Limiting
- Authenticated users: 1000 requests per minute
- Request size limit: 10MB maximum

## Error Handling
All errors follow a consistent format with:
- Error code for programmatic handling
- User-friendly message
- Correlation ID for support
- Detailed validation errors when applicable

See the error codes section below for complete documentation.

post.summary: Triggers AI analysis for a deployment's readiness.
post.description:
This endpoint performs comprehensive AI-powered analysis of a deployment's readiness
for state transition. The analysis includes:
- Evidence completeness assessment
- Approval status verification
- Risk identification
- Compliance checking
- Actionable recommendations

Sample request:

    POST /v1/ai-analysis/deployments/{deploymentId}/analyze?targetState=IN_PROGRESS

Note: This endpoint is subject to strict rate limiting (10 requests per minute)
due to the computational cost of AI analysis.

parameters.name: targetState
parameters.description: Optional target state for transition analysis

429.description: Rate limit exceeded - AI analysis is rate limited due to computational cost

post.summary: Performs AI risk assessment for a deployment.
post.description:
Performs comprehensive risk analysis including:
- Technical risk assessment
- Business impact analysis
- Complexity evaluation
- Historical pattern analysis
- Mitigation strategy recommendations

The AI analyzes deployment characteristics, historical data, and current context
to provide a detailed risk profile with actionable mitigation strategies.

post.summary: Generates AI recommendations for improving a deployment.
post.description:
Generates actionable recommendations based on:
- Current deployment state and issues
- Best practices and patterns
- Historical success factors
- Compliance requirements
- Risk mitigation strategies

Each recommendation includes:
- Clear action description
- Reasoning and justification
- Expected impact
- Implementation guidance

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: The deployment to generate recommendations for

500.description: Internal server error during recommendation generation

get.summary: Gets information about available AI agents.
get.description:
Returns information about all available AI agents including:
- Agent identifier and name
- Version information
- Supported capabilities
- Operational status

This endpoint can be used to discover which AI analysis capabilities
are currently available in the system.

post.summary:
Validates that an AI agent operation is allowed.
This endpoint demonstrates the constraint enforcement for AI agents.
post.description:
This endpoint validates whether a specific AI agent operation is permitted
according to the system's AI governance rules.

AI agents are subject to strict constraints:
- Cannot advance deployment states
- Cannot make approval decisions
- Cannot modify critical deployment data
- Can only provide analysis and recommendations

This ensures human oversight for all critical decisions.

get.summary: Validates if the current user has authority to approve a specific deployment state

post.summary: Requests approval for a deployment to transition to a specific state
Records an approval decision for a pending approval

get.summary: Gets all pending approvals for a deployment
Gets all approvals for a specific deployment state
Checks if a deployment state has sufficient approvals to proceed
Gets the critical gate definition for a lifecycle state
Gets all approvals made by the current user with pagination

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)

post.summary: Receives execution status updates from ARK
Receives completion notifications from ARK

get.summary: Provides deployments ready for execution to ARK

post.summary: Notifies ARK that a deployment is ready for execution

get.summary: Checks the health of ARK integration

post.summary: Creates a new deployment

requestBody.description: The deployment creation request containing title, type, and optional metadata

400.description: Invalid request data or validation failure

get.summary: Gets a paginated list of deployments with optional filtering
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of deployments accessible to the authenticated user.
Results are automatically filtered by the user's client context.

Sample request:

    GET /v1/deployments?state=IN_PROGRESS&page=1&pageSize=20

The response includes pagination metadata with total count, page information, and navigation links.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)

get.summary: Gets a deployment by ID with full details
get.description:
Returns comprehensive deployment details including:
- Basic deployment information (title, type, state)
- Complete transition history
- All submitted evidence
- Approval status
- Active exceptions/waivers

The response includes an ETag header for optimistic concurrency control.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to view this deployment

requestBody.description: The update request containing fields to modify

400.description: Invalid request data or validation failure

403.description: Insufficient permissions to update this deployment

409.description: Deployment is in an immutable state or ETag mismatch

412.description: Precondition failed - If-Match header required or ETag mismatch

delete.summary: Deletes a deployment (soft delete - transitions to CANCELLED state)

post.summary: Requests a state transition for a deployment
Submits evidence for a deployment

get.summary: Gets evidence for a deployment

post.summary: Verifies audit integrity for a specific audit event
Validates a potential state transition without executing it
Creates a new exception request for a deployment

get.summary: Gets all exceptions for a deployment with pagination

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)

post.summary: Validates an exception request without creating it

get.summary: Gets an exception by ID
Gets active exceptions for a deployment

post.summary: Approves an exception request
Denies an exception request

get.summary: Gets the overall health status of the system
get.description:
This endpoint is publicly accessible (no authentication required) to support
load balancer health checks and monitoring systems.

Returns overall system health including:
- Service availability status
- Circuit breaker states
- System version information

Health status values:
- "healthy": All systems operational
- "degraded": Some non-critical services unavailable
- "unhealthy": Critical services unavailable

get.summary: Gets detailed resilience statistics
get.description:
This endpoint requires authentication and provides detailed information about:
- Graceful degradation service statuses
- Circuit breaker states and statistics
- Resilience policy effectiveness

This information is intended for system administrators and operators.

get.summary: Gets circuit breaker status for a specific pipeline
get.description: Requires authentication. Returns detailed circuit breaker information for a specific pipeline.

parameters.name: pipelineName
parameters.description: The name of the resilience pipeline

post.summary: Resets a circuit breaker (for administrative purposes)
post.description:
Requires authentication and admin role. Requests a reset of the circuit breaker for the specified pipeline.
Use with caution as this can affect system resilience.

parameters.name: pipelineName
parameters.description: The name of the resilience pipeline

post.summary: Marks a service as available (for administrative recovery)
post.description:
Requires authentication and admin role. Manually marks a service as available,
overriding automatic health detection. Use for manual recovery scenarios.

get.summary: Gets the current metrics summary

post.summary: Resets all collected metrics (admin only)

get.summary: Gets all data sources available to the current user
get.description:
Returns a list of data sources the user has permission to query based on their roles.
Each data source includes metadata about available fields and configuration.

get.summary: Gets the fields available for a specific data source
get.description:
Returns the list of fields the user can query for the specified data source.
Fields are filtered based on the user's role permissions.

403.description: User does not have access to this data source

post.summary: Executes a user-defined query
post.description:
Executes a query built from user filter selections. The query is:
1. Validated for security (SQL injection prevention)
2. Validated for performance (row limits, WHERE clauses)
3. Checked against user permissions
4. Executed with caching support
5. Audited for compliance

All user values are parameterized to prevent SQL injection.

**Security Features:**
- Rate limiting: 1000 requests per minute for authenticated users
- Request size limit: Maximum 10MB request body
- API key or JWT authentication required
- All queries are parameterized to prevent SQL injection

403.description: User does not have permission to execute this query

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

post.summary: Exports query results to a file
post.description:
Exports query results to CSV, JSON, or Excel format.
Export size is limited to prevent resource exhaustion.
All exports are audited for compliance.

Supported formats:
- CSV: Comma-separated values
- JSON: JSON array of objects
- Excel: XLSX format with formatting

**Security Features:**
- Rate limiting: 1000 requests per minute for authenticated users
- Request size limit: Maximum 10MB request body
- Export size limit: Maximum 50,000 rows

requestBody.description: The export request with query results and format

400.description: Invalid export request or size limit exceeded

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.summary: Gets all query templates available to the current user
get.description:
Returns public templates and templates created by the current user.
Templates provide reusable query structures with parameterization.

post.summary: Saves a new query template
post.description:
Creates a new query template that can be reused with different parameter values.
Templates can be marked as public (available to all users) or private (only creator).

Template SQL must use named parameters (e.g., @paramName) that match the parameter definitions.

403.description: User does not have permission to create templates for this data source

get.summary: Gets a specific query template by ID
get.description:
Returns the full template details including parameters and SQL template.
Users can only access public templates or their own private templates.

403.description: User does not have access to this template

delete.description:
Deletes a query template. Users can only delete their own templates.
Public templates can only be deleted by their creator.

403.description: User does not have permission to delete this template

post.summary: Executes a query template with provided parameters
post.description:
Executes a pre-defined query template with user-provided parameter values.
All parameters are validated and parameterized to prevent SQL injection.
The execution is audited for compliance.

Required parameters must be provided, optional parameters use default values if not specified.

**Security Features:**
- Rate limiting: 1000 requests per minute for authenticated users
- Request size limit: Maximum 10MB request body
- All parameters are parameterized to prevent SQL injection

requestBody.description: The template execution request with parameter values

403.description: User does not have access to this template or data source

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

Provides endpoints for triggering AI analysis while ensuring proper constraints.
